title: workspaces — domain events
description: Domain events THIS module publishes. Payload contract only — no listeners. Subscriber ownership lives in each subscribing module's `listeners.json` so a listener change never mutates the publisher's file. Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write.

events.name: WorkspaceProvisioning
events.when: Fired inside the DB transaction that creates a new Workspace, BEFORE the transaction commits. Used by same-transaction sub-provisioners (default Organization, default Branch, default Region, owner User) so they land in the same commit as the Workspace row.

events.name: WorkspaceProvisioned
events.when: Fired AFTER the provisioning transaction commits. Safe surface for side effects that hit external systems (send-welcome-email, provision-dns, index-search).

events.name: WorkspaceSuspended
events.when: Set `status=suspended` + `suspended_at=now`. Fired by the billing suspension pipeline or a platform-admin action.

events.name: WorkspaceUnsuspended
events.when: Reverse of WorkspaceSuspended. Platform-admin manually unsuspends a workspace. Distinct from WorkspaceResumed — unsuspension is a platform-admin action that requires re-enabling of integrations (webhook / SSO / SCIM); resume is the general lifecycle transition.

events.name: WorkspaceResumed
events.when: Reverse of WorkspaceSuspended. Set `status=active`, clear `suspended_at` + `grace_ends_at`.

events.name: WorkspaceArchived
events.when: Soft-delete the Workspace. Retention job picks it up after 30d for hard-delete.

events.name: WorkspaceErased
events.when: Hard-delete the Workspace after the 30d grace window. GDPR Art. 17 cascade signal — every dependent row must be removed or anonymised. Fired by HardDeleteArchivedWorkspaceJob BEFORE the actual DELETE runs so downstream listeners can produce their compliance-anonymisation entries first.

events.name: WorkspaceSettingsUpdated
events.when: Fires when a Workspace's own settings (branding, sender_config, feature toggles) are updated via PATCH /api/current-workspace. Distinct from settings::SettingsChangeEvent — that's per-field, this is per-workspace summary.

events.name: WorkspaceBrandingUpdated
events.when: Fires when the primary Branding row is updated OR promoted. `before` / `after` reference the full Branding snapshot.

events.name: DomainAdded
events.when: A Domain row is created (customer added a custom domain). DomainObserver generates the verification token BEFORE this event fires.

events.name: DomainVerified
events.when: VerifyDomainDnsJob observes every expected DomainRecord matches. Sets `verified_at`.

events.name: DomainVerificationFailed
events.when: VerifyDomainDnsJob hit `verification_attempts >= 100` without success. Fires once (not per attempt).

events.name: DomainRemoved
events.when: Domain soft-deleted.

events.name: WorkspaceContactAdded
events.when: WorkspaceContact created.

events.name: WorkspaceContactUpdated
events.when: WorkspaceContact fields changed. Fires whenever `verified_at` transitions from null → not-null too.

events.name: WorkspaceContactRemoved
events.when: WorkspaceContact soft-deleted.

events.name: WorkspaceIntegrationConfigured
events.when: WorkspaceIntegration created or `is_active` transitions to true.

events.name: WorkspaceIntegrationSyncFailed
events.when: SyncWorkspaceIntegrationJob left `last_sync_status=failed`. Fires on the first failure; subsequent consecutive failures are collapsed until a success.

events.name: IdentityCreated
events.when: First-ever Identity row for an email under an Application. Subsequent User rows under the same email reuse the Identity, no event.

events.name: IdentityErased
events.when: Cross-workspace Identity row anonymised. Cascades from user::UserErased when the erased User was the last one referencing the Identity for a given Application. Compliance-anonymises the Identity (email hashed, name cleared).
